docs.text: Date: 2026-05-06 Target (whom Sumble is pitching): Five9 (five9.com) Anchor prospect (whom Five9 should pursue): SoFi [NEW LOGO] Recommended recipient at Five9: Jay Lee — Chief Marketing & Growth Officer (multi-thread Matthew Tuckness, CRO + Sam Counterman, SVP Growth Marketing)

docs.title: TL;DR
docs.text: Sumble surfaces a $750K–$1.5M ACV new-logo deal at SoFi invisible to ZoomInfo / Apollo / 6Sense / Demandbase: SoFi runs Genesys on 9 teams with 14 active jobs AND Genesys Cloud on 3 teams with a stalled 2024 migration, AND just posted a Senior AI Engineer to the risk-operations team with Generative AI in scope (Mar 2026). 40–80 fintech / health / banking lookalikes per quarter = ~$30M–$60M qualifying pipeline / quarter, $30M–$60M annualized new ARR at 25% win rate — single-handedly funds Five9's publicly committed >40% AI ARR growth on the $125M base.

docs.title: 1. Business Problem Sumble Solves For Five9
docs.text:
Five9 publicly committed to >40% AI ARR growth in FY26 off a $125M base (Q1 FY25 earnings). 40% on $125M = ~$50M net-new AI ARR for FY26, which at 4× pipeline coverage and 25% win rate requires ~$200M of qualified AI-attached pipeline. Brand-new CMO Jay Lee (Apr 2026, ~30 days in) needs that pipeline math to land before Q3 earnings.

Five9's GTM data stack (mapped via Sumble):

Tool Jobs People Teams Salesforce 283 253 77 Adobe Marketo 22 4 6 Outreach 5 3 6 6Sense 15 1 10 Demandbase 4 0 2 LinkedIn Sales Nav 0 3 0 Clay / Common Room / UserGems / Champify / HG Insights 0 0 0

2023-era stack. 6Sense entrenched at 10 teams, Demandbase decaying (silent since June 2024, zero people). Marketo as primary MAP. No modern signal layer at all — Clay, Common Room, UserGems, Champify all zero. A clean walking-in seat for Sumble to layer above 6Sense + replace dying Demandbase.

Their existing tools tell them who has visited the site and an account intent score. None fingerprint a stalled CCaaS-cloud-migration estate — Genesys-on-prem teams + Genesys Cloud teams running side-by-side, with the cloud migration last touched 18+ months ago — and that pattern is exactly what Five9's cloud-native pitch lands hardest against. Sumble produces it; the existing stack cannot.

docs.title: 2. Anchor Prospect: SoFi [NEW LOGO]
docs.text:
SoFi — public fintech (NASDAQ: SOFI), ~5,544 employees, ~$2B+ revenue, B2C personal finance + B2B-via-Galileo embedded banking. AI / fintech narrative front-and-center, growing contact-center operations across consumer + Galileo lines.

Anchor type rationale: NEW LOGO. SoFi runs Genesys (Five9's primary CCaaS competitor); zero current Five9 deployment.

docs.title: 3. Why SoFi Fits the Trophy Pattern
docs.text:
Five9's named trophies are multi-vertical enterprise: Wyndham (hospitality), Alaska Air (travel), AdventHealth (healthcare), Exact Sciences (healthtech), Kyndryl (BPO). SoFi matches the size band (5K–10K employees), the consumer-facing high-volume contact pattern, and the AI-narrative inflection.

The inflection point: SoFi's Genesys Cloud migration stalled in January 2024 (last Genesys Cloud-tagged job 2024-01-28), but they kept hiring on legacy Genesys (last job 2025-12-23) and just posted a Senior AI Engineer to risk operations with Generative AI in scope (Mar 2026). Stalled cloud migration + new AI ambition on the operations side = the buying window for a clean cloud-native CCaaS with embedded AI. Five9's exact pitch.

docs.title: Anchor deal at SoFi
docs.text:
• ~5,544 employees × estimated 10–14% in CC/operations roles → ~550–775 contact-center seats (matches the 14 active Genesys jobs across 9 teams)
  • Five9 enterprise pricing typically $1,200–2,000 per seat per year on bundled subscription (voice + digital + workforce + AI add-ons)
  • Estimated annual seat math: 550–775 seats × $1,200–2,000 = $660K–$1.55M
  • Estimated anchor ACV: $750K–$1.5M (recurring, lands lower, 12–24 month expansion to AI add-ons)

docs.title: Net-new lookalike TAM for the "stalled Genesys-Cloud + active AI hiring" play
docs.text:
• ~40–80 fintech / healthcare / banking enterprises per quarter run Genesys or NICE AND have an active AI Engineering / NLP / Conversational AI hire on operations or risk teams (estimated from Sumble query base of ~290 US fintech/healthcare/banking 1K–15K-employee orgs on Genesys/NICE)
  • Avg ACV at this segment: $500K–$1M
  • 60 lookalike accounts × $750K avg = $45M qualifying pipeline per quarter
  • 25% win rate = $11.25M new ARR / quarter = $45M annualized

→ $45M from a single Sumble-surfaced play covers ~90% of Five9's stated >40% AI growth target ($50M) — and that is conservative before counting expansion within already-on-Five9 customers (the second play).

docs.title: 5. Strategic Frame — Why This Account, Why Now
Trigger 1 (the bullseye): SoFi's Genesys Cloud migration is stalled
docs.text: Last Genesys Cloud-tagged job at SoFi was 2024-01-28. Last legacy Genesys-tagged job was 2025-12-23. That's 18+ months of no cloud migration progress while legacy Genesys keeps hiring — a textbook "we got stuck on the migration and kept the lights on with on-prem" pattern. The buying window opens when leadership decides to pull the plug and start fresh on a cloud-native vendor, which Five9 is and Genesys Cloud (as a migration target from legacy Genesys) was not.

docs.title: Trigger 2: Senior AI Engineer hire on risk operations with Generative AI in scope
docs.text:
Person Title Start (newly posted) Senior AI Engineer 2026-03-10 (Frisco, TX) (Mar 2026 hire on independent risk organization team) scope: Generative AI, Microservices Migration, Digital Transformation —

This is the operational AI ambition signal. Five9's pitch is "cloud-native CCaaS with embedded AI" — exactly the architecture this hire would gravitate to. Risk-ops teams typically pull contact-center decisions when fraud/identity workflows get hit.

docs.title: Trigger 3: Public AI / fintech narrative + Galileo expansion
docs.text: SoFi's earnings narrative leans heavily on AI + Galileo's B2B embedded banking. Both flow through contact-center decisions: consumer-side AI for SoFi.com, B2B-side scaling for Galileo customer support. Both are Five9's wheelhouse.

docs.title: Why the window will close
docs.text: Once a stalled cloud migration crosses 24 months, leadership typically either restarts on the same vendor (sunk-cost choice) or commits hard to a fresh vendor. SoFi is at month 18 in May 2026. Six-month window before the path-dependence locks in.

docs.title: 6. Buyer at SoFi (per Five9's ICP — Customer Service / VP Operations leader)
docs.text:
🥇 VP / SVP Customer Operations or Member Services at SoFi (verify via FindPeople before naming). Owns the seat count + platform decision + AI rollout on the consumer line.

🥈 VP Risk Operations / Director Fraud & Risk (the Senior AI Engineer hiring manager — likely champion since they scope the Generative AI work that Five9's IVA suite plugs into).

🥉 CIO / VP IT (multi-thread for the migration-from-Genesys financial + technical frame; SoFi's stalled cloud migration sits in their org).

docs.title: 7. What Sumble Surfaced (That ZoomInfo / Apollo / 6Sense / Demandbase Cannot)
docs.text:
• The stalled cloud migration timestamp. ZoomInfo and 6Sense show "SoFi uses Genesys" — true for the last decade. Sumble shows: Genesys legacy still hiring (last job Dec 2025), Genesys Cloud went silent in Jan 2024, the gap between last-modern-job-and-last-legacy-job is 22 months. That is the stall signal. Firmographic / intent tools cannot produce it because they don't track the last-job-on-tech delta.
  • The team-level AI hiring on risk ops. Apollo can return the Senior AI Engineer's title. Sumble flags the team assignment ("independent risk organization") and the project scope tags ("Generative AI", "Microservices Migration") that mark this as a CCaaS-adjacent decision point — not a generic ML role.
  • The trophy fingerprint match. 6Sense and Demandbase show category intent. Sumble compares SoFi's full deployment fingerprint (Genesys legacy + stalled cloud + AI-on-risk-ops + 5K-emp consumer fintech) against Five9's named trophy roster (Wyndham, Alaska Air, AdventHealth, Exact Sciences) and ranks the structural match before a rep ever opens the account.
  • The migration-window timing math. UserGems flags job changes. None tells you a stalled migration is at month 18 of a likely 24-month decision window. Sumble's last-job-on-tech timestamps drive that calculation.

docs.title: 8. The Pitch Five9 Should Run
docs.text:
Lead: "SoFi has been on Genesys forever, kicked off a cloud migration in late 2023, and went silent on it 18 months ago — while keeping the lights on with on-prem hires. Just posted a Senior AI Engineer to risk operations with Generative AI in scope. That is the buying window, and it closes around Q4 2026. Want a 30-minute walk-through of how we'd run the cloud-native + AI pitch with AdventHealth and Exact Sciences as the proof points?"

Proof points: Wyndham, Alaska Air, AdventHealth, Exact Sciences, Kyndryl from five9.com/customers. Five9's 68% YoY AI revenue growth + the >40% AI growth FY26 guidance.

CTA: 30-min discovery with VP Customer Operations + VP Risk Operations. Pilot scope: 90-day Five9 IVA on the risk-ops fraud-flow with hand-off to existing legacy Genesys for non-AI flows. Multi-thread to CIO before the multi-year platform contract.

docs.title: Methodology — how Sumble's MCP found this
docs.text:
1. GetMyCompanyProfile (free) — grounded Sumble's persona/play context
  2. WebFetch [five9.com] — extracted trophy customers, AI ARR commitment, FY26 guidance
  3. EnrichOrganization on five9.com filtered to Sumble competitors + complementary tools — mapped 6Sense (15/10) entrenched, Demandbase decaying, no modern signal layer
  4. FindPeople on five9.com — surfaced Jay Lee (CMO, ~30 days), Matthew Tuckness (CRO Jul 2025), Sam Counterman (SVP Growth Marketing Oct 2025), Christian Phillips (VP Sales Ops Aug 2025)
  5. EnrichPerson on Andy Dignan (President) — confirmed Five9 email convention firstname.lastname@five9.com
  6. SearchTechnologies('genesys') + SearchTechnologies('nice cxone') — confirmed slugs genesys-cloud, nice-cxone
  7. FindOrganizations with industry IN ('Finance and Insurance','Health Care and Social Assistance','Accommodation and Food Services') AND technology IN ('genesys-cloud','genesys','nice-cxone') AND employee_count 1000-15000 — surfaced SoFi (account_score 20.96, "Good")
  8. EnrichOrganization on sofi.com — confirmed Genesys (14 jobs/9 teams) + Genesys Cloud (1 job/3 teams stalled) + AWS Connect (zero footprint)
  9. FindJobs on sofi.com with AI Engineer / Machine Learning + NLP/Generative AI tags — surfaced the Mar 2026 Senior AI Engineer hire on risk operations

Total elapsed time: ~6 minutes. ~232 credits.

The pre-IPO marketing reorg under Veronica Servantez (SVP Product Marketing, Apr 2025) and the new Demand Hub being stood up under VP Integrated Marketing Americas exist precisely to deliver this number.

Genesys's GTM data stack (mapped via Sumble):

Tool Jobs People Teams Salesforce 1,044 650 243 Salesloft 174 11 22 6Sense 108 2 21 Outreach 106 11 10 LinkedIn Sales Nav 87 18 10 Adobe Marketo 33 15 11 ZoomInfo 17 7 2 UserGems 4 0 1 Demandbase 2 1 3 Gong 2 1 2 Clay / Common Room / Champify / HG Insights 0 0 0

Heavy stack — 6Sense entrenched at 21 teams, Salesloft + Outreach as primary engagement layer, Marketo as MAP, Salesforce massive at 243 teams. UserGems silent for 8 months (zero people, last job Sep 2025), Demandbase dead for 18 months (zero active hiring). The decay zone is real and Sumble walks into the empty UserGems / Demandbase seat. docs.text:
Genesys's named trophies are F500 enterprise across BFSI / Telco / Hospitality / Travel: HSBC, Vodafone, Visa, Best Buy Canada, BNP Paribas, Virgin Atlantic. Mastercard is the direct fintech parallel to Visa — same global F500 payment-network shape, same multi-region contact-center structure, same regulatory + compliance pressure profile.

The inflection point: Mastercard's Avaya footprint is mid-migration (22 active jobs across 13 teams as of January 2026). F500 contact-center modernization typically spans 24–36 months from "we are evaluating" to "we have signed a multi-year cloud contract." Mastercard is in the active-evaluation window, and Genesys's PE-to-IPO narrative needs F500 logos like this one signed before the listing.

docs.text: Sumble surfaces a $200K–$400K ACV new-logo deal at YETI invisible to ZoomInfo / Apollo / 6Sense / Clay: fragmented CCaaS stack (Salesforce Service Cloud on 5 teams + Zendesk on 6 teams, 23 active jobs across both), Senior Director Customer Experience Stephanie Stafford 2 years into building the CX function at a premium-D2C trophy lookalike (TUMI / UGG / HOKA / Tecovas). 30–50 lookalikes per quarter = ~$6M–$15M qualifying pipeline / quarter, $6M–$15M annualized new ARR at 25% win rate — and Gladly's CMO Ali Fazal (Mar 2026, ~2 months in) needs net-new pipeline to prove the gladly.com → gladly.ai rebrand pays.

docs.title: 1. Business Problem Sumble Solves For Gladly
docs.text:
Gladly just rebranded gladly.com → gladly.ai (308 redirect verified) — they have bet the company on "the only customer experience AI built for LTV" positioning against Zendesk Fin and Salesforce Service Cloud Einstein. Brand-new CEO Charlie Besecker (Jun 2025) and brand-new CMO Ali Fazal (Mar 2026, ~2 months in) need the rebrand to translate into net-new pipeline at premium D2C accounts within the first two quarters or the narrative stalls.

No public ARR target disclosed. Inferred segment math: Gladly competes against Zendesk and Salesforce Service Cloud at premium D2C. Their named trophy customers — TUMI, Ulta, UGG, HOKA, Crate & Barrel, Tory Burch, Lovesac, StockX, Bombas, Rothy's, The Farmer's Dog, Tecovas, BARK — sit in the $50M–$2B revenue band. ~30–50 premium-D2C lookalikes per quarter run a competing CX platform with a recent CX leadership move; at $150K–$300K avg ACV that's $4.5M–$15M qualifying pipeline / quarter from a single play.

Tool Jobs People Teams Salesforce 28 36 10 Outreach 15 7 7 Adobe Marketo 13 4 4 Gong 8 4 4 6Sense 7 0 2 ZoomInfo 5 4 4 LinkedIn Sales Nav 4 2 2 Apollo 0 1 0 Clay 1 1 1 Common Room / UserGems / Champify / HG Insights 0 0 0

Lean modern stack — Marketo + Outreach + Salesforce core, 6Sense and ZoomInfo are minimum-footprint shelfware, and there is no modern signal layer at all (Clay barely seeded, Common Room/UserGems/Champify zero). Walking into the dead 6Sense seat is the cleanest displacement available.

Their existing tools tell them who has form-filled and an intent score. None fingerprint a fragmented CCaaS deployment + a 2-year-tenured CX leader still hiring against a competing platform — and that pattern is exactly what Gladly's MQL-enrichment + ABM-list-build plays need to fire reliably.

docs.text: Sumble surfaces a $500K–$2M churn-prevent play at Leidos invisible to ZoomInfo, 6Sense, or Demandbase: 2,694 active Splunk job posts, 172 Splunk people, and 543 Splunk teams against just 167 SolarWinds teams — your largest enterprise customer is being modernized against you in real time, and a new CTO joined Leidos two months ago. ~14 federal-contractor lookalikes in the install base = $7M–$14M defendable ARR per quarter from a single play, ~10–18% of SolarWinds' annual net-new subscription ARR target under PE ownership.

docs.title: 1. Business Problem Sumble Solves For SolarWinds
docs.text:
Anchor to public goal. Last public SolarWinds metrics (FY2024 release, March 2025): Total ARR $729M (+7% YoY), Subscription ARR $311.7M (+34% YoY). The gap is the entire game — SolarWinds is not adding meaningful net-new ARR, they're converting their installed base from perpetual to subscription. Every defection to Splunk / Datadog / Dynatrace is direct ARR loss with no offset.

Under PE ownership, retention IS the strategy. Turn/River Capital took SolarWinds private in February 2025 for $4.4B ($18.50/share). The PE thesis on a heavily-levered IT-monitoring legacy is cash extraction + cross-sell, not aggressive net-new. #1 KPI: gross retention rate. #2 KPI: subscription conversion velocity. Quarterly scoreboard.

The problem with their current GTM stack. SolarWinds' GTM tooling — caveat: numbers below partly conflated with N-able — is built for net-new outbound, not customer-base retention:

Tool Jobs People Teams Salesforce 830 338 116 Outreach 150 9 22 Adobe Marketo 109 9 27 6Sense 52 4 8 Grafana (internal observability!) 37 8 6 ZoomInfo 29 3 9 Mutiny 11 0 4 LinkedIn Sales Nav 9 11 5 Qualified 8 0 4

None of these tools tell SolarWinds which of their ~300K customers is hiring against them right now. 6Sense's intent data points at accounts shopping toward you. ZoomInfo enriches contacts you already know. Mutiny personalizes web; Qualified runs chat; Marketo runs marketing automation. None surface the team-level defection signal.

Sumble's wedge: customer-base early-warning system. Sumble's data — 543 Splunk teams, 449 Grafana teams, 112 Dynatrace job posts, 41 Datadog jobs at Leidos — is the signal layer the rest of their stack cannot produce.

docs.title: 2. Anchor Prospect: Leidos [EXPANSION-DEFENSE / CHURN-PREVENT]
docs.text:
Leidos is a $16B fed contractor and integrator HQ'd in Reston, VA. ~31,000 employees. Industry: Professional Services / Defense / Government IT integration. Top SolarWinds customer signal in Sumble's data: 1,026 active job posts mention SolarWinds, 46 people list SolarWinds in skills, 167 teams reference SolarWinds tooling.

Anchor type rationale: Default [NEW LOGO] doesn't fit — SolarWinds already has Leidos. The right tag is [EXPANSION-DEFENSE / CHURN-PREVENT]: Leidos is on the cusp of a structural shift to Splunk/ELK, and the SolarWinds CSM motion can save (and expand) the account if it acts now.

docs.title: 3. Why Leidos Fits the Trophy Pattern
docs.text:
SolarWinds' commercial customer base has been bleeding to Datadog/Splunk/Dynatrace since 2020. The federal contractor base has been their last sticky segment — security clearances, FedRAMP compliance lock-in, and ATO migration costs make defection slower and more expensive.

But the lock-in is breaking. The FedRAMP marketplace now has Splunk Cloud (high), Splunk on-prem with FedRAMP-aligned configs, and Elastic Cloud as authorized alternatives. The decade-old SolarWinds entrenchment is now a liability rather than a moat.

Leidos is the ideal trophy pattern: - Largest fed contractor by SolarWinds team count (167) — top of the customer pyramid - 14 federal-contractor lookalikes also in the install base (General Dynamics, Northrop Grumman, Peraton, SAIC, Raytheon, KBR, ManTech, HII, CACI, Jacobs — almost all VA/MD-based defense primes) - SolarWinds already has a dedicated "FED Renewals Conversion Sales" team in Reston, VA (active job post 2026-04-30) — they are already focused on this segment; the playbook they need is the signal layer, not a strategy pivot - Leidos has been a SolarWinds customer for years → relationship is non-cold; this is win-back with active-customer status, not pure outbound

docs.text:
Leidos has 543 Splunk teams vs 167 SolarWinds teams — a 3.25× ratio. This is not a "sometime in the future" defection — it has already happened operationally. The remaining question is whether the contract follows the operational shift in the next 6 quarters.

docs.title: Trigger 2: Recent CTO hire at Leidos (March 2026)
docs.text: Pallavi Sandhiya joined as Chief Technology Officer in Herndon, VA, in March 2026 — two months ago. New CTOs in fed contracting almost always trigger tooling reviews within their first 90–180 days. The window opens now. (Source: Sumble person record.)

docs.title: Trigger 3: Turn/River PE ownership at SolarWinds (since Feb 2025)
docs.text: Quarterly retention scoreboards under PE ownership mean SolarWinds GTM has months, not years, to put a defensive motion on every Leidos-shape account. Turn/River bought $4.4B of customer-base cash flow; their playbook does not tolerate an 18-month "monitor the situation" approach.

docs.title: Why the window will close
docs.text:
1. Leidos's next observability budget cycle. Federal fiscal year starts Oct 1; modernization budgets typically lock in Q3. SolarWinds has ~5 months to pre-empt with a retention-tier renewal.
  2. Splunk's parallel push. Splunk is hiring against the same 14 fed-contractor accounts (visible in Sumble). Once Splunk closes renewal/expansion deals there, the legacy SolarWinds contracts get cancelled at next renewal.

CISO multi-thread: Antonio Gray — Chief Information Security Officer, DC (since 2019-02). Long tenure, owns the security/compliance angle that historically locked SolarWinds in. The right person to test whether the FedRAMP lock-in still holds. LinkedIn

docs.title: 7. What Sumble Surfaced (That ZoomInfo / Apollo / 6Sense / Clay / Demandbase Cannot)
docs.text:
• Team-level competitor density. ZoomInfo and 6Sense show "Leidos uses Splunk." Sumble shows "Leidos has 543 Splunk teams to your 167 SolarWinds teams" — that's the inflection signal. ZoomInfo cannot surface team-level granularity; intent providers cannot quantify the operational mass.
  • Multi-product observability stack texture. Sumble surfaced 7 modern observability tools in active use at Leidos (Splunk, ELK, Grafana, Elasticsearch, Datadog, Dynatrace, New Relic). That texture is the proof — modernization is portfolio-wide, not pilot-stage. None of this is in 6Sense's tech intent.
  • Hiring-velocity defection signal. 2,694 active Splunk job posts at Leidos vs ~1,026 SolarWinds-related posts (mostly maintenance/admin roles). Splunk is hiring offensively; SolarWinds skills are being maintained, not built. Apollo and Demandbase do not surface offensive-vs-defensive hiring patterns.
  • Recent CTO change tied to active hiring pattern. Pallavi Sandhiya joined March 2026 while the Splunk hiring is at 2,694 active posts. That correlation = budget review window. Demandbase intent data is account-level and time-aggregated; Sumble surfaces person-and-time level signal.
  • The fed-contractor lookalike list. ~14 SolarWinds-customer fed contractors at 5K+ employees, ranked by tech-team density. Sumble can rerun this query weekly and surface deltas — a retention dashboard the rest of the GTM stack cannot produce.

docs.title: 8. The Pitch SolarWinds Should Run
docs.text:
Lead: "Anil — Pallavi just joined as CTO last month, and Leidos has 543 Splunk teams to our 167 SolarWinds teams across your programs. We have a portfolio retention package built for the federal modernization cycle. 15 minutes to walk through what changes for the next renewal?"

Proof points: SolarWinds Hybrid Cloud Observability for federal customers (FedRAMP-aligned). Specific renewal/consolidation case studies of fed primes who unified SolarWinds + Splunk into a tiered observability motion (if those exist; otherwise lead with the math).

CTA: 30-day retention pilot scoped to one Leidos program where Splunk is currently displacing. Multi-thread to Robert Franceschini at Group level + Antonio Gray on the security side.

docs.title: Methodology — how Sumble's MCP found this
docs.text:
1. GetMyCompanyProfile (free) — refreshed Sumble's persona/play context.
  2. WebFetch solarwinds.com — failed (403, Cloudflare). Wikipedia + WebSearch used as fallback.
  3. EnrichOrganization on solarwinds.com filtered to GTM competitors + observability competitors — mapped GTM data stack and surfaced internal observability footprint. Caveat: data conflated with N-able. 100 credits.
  4. FindJobs on solarwinds.com filtered to GTM functions (limit 30) — surfaced the FED Renewals Conversion Sales role (Reston VA, 2026-04-30, key signal) and the offshore-heavy customer-success motion (Philippines). 60 credits.
  5. FindPeople on solarwinds.com filtered to Director+ in GTM functions (limit 40) — built the persona shortlist. EnrichPerson disambiguated solarwinds.com vs n-able.com via email domain (40 credits across 4 reveals) — only Erin Viator Blank and Jenne Barbour confirmed at SolarWinds proper; Adam Hunt and Stuti Jhunjhunwala at N-able. 40 credits.
  6. WebSearch SolarWinds 2026 earnings — surfaced Turn/River acquisition (Feb 2025), FY24 ARR ($729M total / $311.7M subscription), no 2025 guidance available.
  7. Phase 6.5 anchor query pre-validation — filter set documented as technology EQ 'solarwinds' AND employee_count IN ('5000-') AND hq_location EQ 'US'. Eric pre-approved with "up to you" on anchor selection.
  8. SearchTechnologies "solarwinds" — confirmed solarwinds slug (22,061 mentions). 1 credit.
  9. FindOrganizations (Phase 7) — surfaced 14 federal-contractor anchor candidates. Top: Leidos (1,026 SolarWinds-tech jobs, Account Score 15.17). 75 credits.
  10. EnrichOrganization on leidos.com filtered to observability tools — confirmed Splunk dominance (2,694 jobs / 172 people / 543 teams) + 6 other modern tools active. 35 credits.
  11. FindPeople on leidos.com filtered to Director+ in Engineering / IT — identified Anil Bansal (Portfolio CTO) + Robert Franceschini (Group CTO) + Pallavi Sandhiya (recent CTO hire, 2026-03). 25 credits.
  12. EnrichPerson on Erin Viator Blank + Jenne Barbour — verified emails for Gmail drafts.

Total elapsed: ~30 minutes interactive. ~376 Sumble credits spent (well within budget; 9,524 remaining).

docs.title: Xero NZ Mid-Market — Outbound Use Case Brief
docs.text: For: Rob Borland (Director of Sales, NZ — Xero) From: Eric Fitzgerald (Account Executive — Sumble) Built: 2026-05-06 Lead anchor: Orion Health [NEW LOGO]

docs.title: TL;DR
docs.text: NZ mid-market is where Xero's FY28 "more than double FY25 revenue" target gets built. Orion Health (Auckland, ~500 emp, healthcare SaaS) is sitting on a textbook displacement window: 24 NetSuite jobs open in 2026, post-HEALWELL-AI acquisition integration in motion, near-zero existing Xero footprint. Sumble found the trigger ZoomInfo can't see; replicate the play across 7 NZ mid-market lookalikes for ~$2–4M annualized new-logo TAM.

docs.title: 1. Business problem Sumble solves for Xero NZ
docs.text:
Sukhinder Singh Cassidy publicly committed to doubling FY25 revenue by FY28 and quadrupling TAM from NZD 750B → NZD 3.3T via AI. ANZ ARPU +12% YoY confirms mid-market is the lever — micro is saturated.

Rob inherited the NZ direct seat in Feb 2026 (3 months in). His mandate: convert Xero's mid-market push into NZ pipeline.

The gap in Xero's stack: - Salesforce: 335 jobs / 168 people / 107 teams (huge execution layer) - Salesforce Marketing Cloud: 118 jobs / 36 teams (heavy ABM/nurture investment) - Outreach: 3 jobs, Gong: 1 job / 4 teams - ZoomInfo: 1 person, 0 jobs. Clay, 6sense, Common Room, HG Insights, Demandbase: zero footprint.

Translation: Xero is running mid-market plays out of Salesforce with no project-trigger or org-graph signal layer feeding the funnel. Rob's reps don't have a way to find the moment a target's finance stack opens. That's what Sumble surfaces.

docs.text: Field Value HQ Grafton, Auckland, NZ Employees 492 Industry Healthcare SaaS (Amadeus platform — 450 customers, 25 countries) Parent HEALWELL AI (acquisition received regulatory approval, NZD $175M) Current finance stack NetSuite (24 jobs / 16 teams, last job 2026-03-17), trace SAP / Workday / QuickBooks Xero footprint 1 person mention, 0 jobs, 0 teams — net-new logo Sumble URL https://sumble.com/l/org/wQUVJD6rg46zW5

docs.title: 3. Why Orion Health fits Xero's NZ trophy pattern
docs.text:
• Mid-market sweet spot. ~500 employees — bigger than micro, smaller than enterprise ERP. Xero's NZ mid-market product lands here cleanly.
  • NZ-HQ, software-savvy. Auckland-grown SaaS company. They understand cloud-native tooling and will evaluate on TCO + integration speed, not procurement inertia.
  • Post-acquisition decision window. HEALWELL acquired them in 2025. Post-M&A is the #1 trigger for system-of-record consolidation. The window is open now and will close once parent integration locks in.
  • Active 2026 NetSuite hiring proves they're spending on finance ops — not pausing, not cost-cutting. Budget is allocated; the question is whether it stays with NetSuite.

docs.title: 4. Financial frame (segment-by-segment)
docs.text:
Orion Health alone (the anchor): - NetSuite at ~500 emp scale in ANZ ≈ NZD 200–400K/yr list (mid-market ERP pricing). - Xero displacement opportunity = core financial close + payroll + AP automation = ~NZD 80–150K ARR (net-new logo, conservative band). - Multi-year + payments cross-sell: 1.5–2× over 3 years.

NZ mid-market lookalike segment (top 7 from Sumble pull, full list in §9): - Combined NetSuite / MYOB displacement TAM at this filter = ~NZD 2–4M ARR new-logo. - Math: 7 candidates × NZD 80–150K ARR midpoint × 30–50% qualification rate × 25–40% win rate over 18 months.

For Rob's seat: This is one play, one filter, one geo. Run it monthly with refreshed signals = ~3 anchors per quarter, ~12/year. That's how a Sales Director closes a coverage gap.

The signal that earns Rob the meeting isn't firmographic — it's signal density on a specific technology + project + role intersection over time. ZoomInfo says "Orion Health is on NetSuite" (so does their website). Sumble says:

This is the methodology proof. We didn't buy a list. We built the map.

docs.text:
Subject line for Sean Church: Post-HEALWELL — quick read on your NetSuite footprint

Opening hypothesis (Rob's first email — Sumble built the data, Rob delivers the line):

Sean — congrats on the close. Saw the team has 24 finance and ERP roles open in 2026 and HEALWELL integration in motion. Worth 20 minutes to compare what Xero looks like at your scale?

Voice rules: plain text, specific signal (24 jobs, 16 teams), no procurement language, low-friction time ask.
